meaning: n.刮,擦,擦痕,刮擦声,困境vi.刮掉,擦掉,刮出刺耳声vt.刮,擦,擦伤,挖成
examples: I might scrape through the exam if I'm lucky.
We managed to scrape together eight volunteers.
I can just scrape by on what my parents give me.
"If you paint a new house, you need to scrape off the old paint, " says Rabiner.
Switch to a rubber spatula, scrape down the bowl and stir in the chopped chocolate.
